title: Map a Lobbying Campaign
description: Map the actors, access points, messages, and integrity risks around a real political influence effort.
whyItMatters: This project turns lobbying from a vague suspicion into a concrete process of actors, incentives, access, and transparency risks.

steps.title: Choose an issue
steps.description: Pick a live or recent issue where organised interests clearly tried to move government.

steps.title: List actors and interests
steps.description: Identify corporations, NGOs, unions, industry bodies, ministers, departments, committees, and media actors.

steps.title: Map channels and timing
steps.description: Show where influence entered: submissions, meetings, media, committees, coalition work.

steps.title: Flag integrity risks
steps.description: Note where transparency, access asymmetry, or corruption risk appears.

deliverables: A short note on the main integrity risk and underrepresented interest

rubric.description: Does the map include both formal and informal influence channels?
Does it distinguish legitimate influence from corrupted or opaque influence?
